messages.content:
What is the name of the commonly used remote computer Remote Control Protocol?
VNC
TCP
RNB
UDP
OLX
ABC
VNC

What is a proxy server usually used for?
To build an encrypted tunnel between different networks
To ensure authorization of users of streaming services
To make indirect requests to other servers on the network
To protect private networks from attacks from the global network
To make indirect requests to other servers on the network.

What is the name of the Apple App for remote connection to the Mac OS desktop?
desktop Plus
Mac RDP
Apple Remote Desktop
iMac Desktop
Apple Remote Desktop.

How does display brightness affect battery life on mobile devices?
Does not affect battery life due to the features of modern displays
When using a TFT display, excessive brightness reduces the operating time, while when using an OLED display, it does not affect the operating time.
The higher the brightness, the shorter the battery life.
The higher the brightness, the shorter the battery life.

Which version of Windows supports only outgoing RDP connections?
Pro (Professional)
Home (Home)
Education (for educational institutions)
Enterprise (Corporate)
Windows Home (Home) supports only outgoing RDP connections.

What are the same features for iOS and Android smartphones?
The user can install the application from a pre-downloaded installation file
User data can be stored in the cloud
The fingerprint scanner can be integrated under the display
Voice assistant allows you to use your smartphone more conveniently
You can determine your phone's location using the online service
There is a special messenger for users of this platform
User data can be stored in the cloud, voice assistant allows you to use your smartphone more conveniently, and you can determine your phone's location using the online service.

What is SSH?
Protocol for remote control of PC component overclocking functions
Standard protocol for Remote Desktop in Linux
Ecosystem protocol for Apple devices
Protocol used for remote connection to the command-line interface
SSH is a protocol used for remote connection to the command-line interface.

What is BranchCache technology used for?
For caching user profiles on a network with a domain controller
As an additional browser cache in Ram
To optimize network bandwidth by managing files
As a proxy server built into Windows Enterprise
BranchCache technology is used to optimize network bandwidth by managing files.

3 What is the purpose of VPN technology?
To combine different private networks into one large virtual network
To provide internet access to virtual machines in hypervisors
To speed up access to large sites
To get a public IP address for a computer located behind Nat
The purpose of VPN technology is to combine different private networks into one large virtual network, allowing secure remote access to the network from anywhere in the world.

What are the two main modes of Remote Assistant (remote help) in Windows?
Control (Full control)
Administration (Administration)
View (View)
Guest mode
The two main modes of Remote Assistant (remote help) in Windows are view mode and control mode.

Select the OS types that Windows 10 Pro belongs to
Multi-user
Many cluster on
Multiprocessor
Multitasking
Many domain on

Windows 10 Pro belongs to the following OS types:

- Multi-user: Yes
- Many clusters on: No
- Multiprocessor: Yes
- Multitasking: Yes
- Many domain on: Yes

What does Windows 10 Home, Pro, and Education editions have in common?
Ability to join a domain
The same operating system kernel
Support for BranchCache technology
Built - in support for running Mac OS X applications
Windows 10 Home, Pro, and Education editions have the same operating system kernel.

What can I configure on the Startup tab in Windows Task Manager?
Enable automatic virtualization activation when the hypervisor starts
Configure System settings for running the operating system kernel
Enable or disable program startup with Windows startup
On the Startup tab in Windows Task Manager, you can configure the programs that are enabled or disabled during system startup.

What services can be implemented in your home router?
VPN technology
Domain controller
DHCP server
QoS Function

The services that can be implemented in a home router are DHCP server (for allocating IP addresses to devices on the network), QoS function (for prioritizing network traffic from different devices and applications), and VPN technology (for accessing the home network securely from outside locations). A home router does not typically serve as a domain controller.

What is Windows 365?
Computer in the cloud
Cloud platform for running applications for ARM
ITaaS type service
Remote Desktop service

Windows 365 is a new cloud-based service from Microsoft that provides a full Windows 10 or Windows 11 operating system environment, including apps and data, to users on any device with an internet connection. It can be considered as a type of ITaaS (IT as a Service) that enables users to access a virtualized version of Windows on-demand without the need for hardware, infrastructure, or maintenance.

What is the advantage of a hybrid type 1+hypervisor?
It has the ability to run 128-bit operating systems
Supports guest OS Ram compression
Combines the advantages of Type 1 and type 2 hypervisors
Allows you to run more than 4 VMs simultaneously

The advantage of a hybrid type 1+ hypervisor is that it combines the advantages of both Type 1 and Type 2 hypervisors. This allows for better performance, security, and management capabilities compared to using just one type of hypervisor. Hybrid hypervisors also typically support features like live migration and virtual machine replication, allowing VMs to be moved between physical hosts without downtime or data loss.

What is the difference between different versions of Windows 10?
Additional functionality
License price
Driver support
DDR4 support

The main differences between different versions of Windows 10 are in the additional functionality they provide and their licensing restrictions. The more expensive versions (such as Windows 10 Pro and Enterprise) offer additional features like remote desktop access, BitLocker encryption, and group policy management, while the lower-priced Home edition has more limited functionality. Driver support and DDR4 support are not typically major differentiators between different editions of Windows 10, as these are usually supported across most versions.

What is the difference between different versions of Windows 10? (only select numbers)
1. Additional functionality
2. License price
3. Driver support
4. DDR4 support

The main differences between different versions of Windows 10 are additional functionality and license price. While driver support and DDR4 support are not typically major differentiators between different editions of Windows 10.

What is a print server? (only select numbers)
1. A virtual printer that is installed on a public server
2. Software or device that makes the printer available on the network
3. A printer that is installed on a type 1 hypervisor
4. A service that allows you to copy a virtual printer to the cloud

A print server is a software or device that makes the printer available on the network. It is not a virtual printer installed on a public server, a printer installed on a type 1 hypervisor, or a service that allows you to copy a virtual printer to the cloud.

What objects can NTFS file system compression be applied to?
Disks
Bytes
Files
Groups
Domains
Folders
NTFS file system compression can be applied to individual files and folders.

Select the correct statements about the workgroup with Windows computers
User credentials are stored on a dedicated server
All computers are equal
The "Guest" account is used by the administrator to configure the network
Windows does not identify network users by SID

The correct statements about the workgroup with Windows computers are:

- All computers are equal
- Windows does not identify network users by SID

The statement "User credentials are stored on a dedicated server" is true for a domain, not a workgroup. And the statement "The 'Guest' account is used by the administrator to configure the network" is incorrect, as the Guest account is typically used for limited access for non-authenticated users.

What is inheritance rights in Windows?
Ability to upgrade to new versions of Windows while saving settings
Child objects have the same rights settings as parent objects
When access to sites is maintained regardless of the network that the PC is connected to

Inheritance rights in Windows refers to the concept that child objects have the same rights and permissions settings as their parent objects. This simplifies the process of managing access to resources, as permissions can be assigned at a high level and automatically passed down to all child objects without having to set them manually. Inheritance is commonly used in managing access to files, folders, and other resources in the Windows operating system. The concept is not related to upgrading to new versions of Windows or maintaining access to sites regardless of network connections.
Choose answer from given text
I'm sorry, but I don't see any text or question to choose an answer from. Could you please provide more information or ask a specific question?

Choose right answer by number

1. Ability to upgrade to new versions of Windows while saving settings
2.Child objects have the same rights settings as parent objects
3. When access to sites is maintained regardless of the network that the PC is connected to

The correct statement among the given options is: 

2. Child objects have the same rights settings as parent objects.

What is the difference between HARDLINK and SOFTLINK in NTFS?
Windows 10 only supports soft links
Soft link points to folder, hard link points to file
Hard links indicate an area on disk, soft links indicate a file
Soft links cannot be used on SSDs

The main difference between HARDLINK and SOFTLINK in NTFS is that hard links refer to the same physical file on disk and are indistinguishable from the original file, while soft links (also called symbolic links) are pointers to another file or folder and can be used to reference files or directories on different volumes or partitions.

More specifically:

- Hard links point directly to a file on disk and can be created only for files, not folders. They reference the inode of a file, making it appear as if there are multiple file paths to the same file. Hard links cannot reference a file or folder on a different volume or partition.
- Soft links (symbolic links) point to another file or folder by referencing a file path, rather than the inode. They can be created for both files and directories and can reference a file or folder on a different volume or partition.

Note that Windows 10 does support both hard and soft links in NTFS. Also, the type of link used does not depend on whether the storage device is an SSD or not.

Choose answers by numbers

1. Windows 10 only supports soft links
2.Soft link points to folder, hard link points to file
3. Hard links indicate an area on disk, soft links indicate a file
4. Soft links cannot be used on SSDs

The correct statements among the given options are:

2. Soft link points to folder, hard link points to file
3. Hard links indicate an area on disk, soft links indicate a file

The statements "Windows 10 only supports soft links" and "Soft links cannot be used on SSDs" are incorrect.

What is the main reason why you can't install any OS on your mobile device? (Choose answers by numbers)

1. Lack of support for high-definition displays

2. Blocked bootloader

3. Insufficient amount of flash memory in the device

The main reason why you can't install any OS on your mobile device is 2. Blocked bootloader. 

While reasons such as lack of support for high-definition displays and insufficient amount of flash memory may affect the performance or functionality of an OS on a mobile device, a blocked bootloader prevents users from installing custom ROMs or operating systems on the device.

Prove the relevance of mobile operating systems:
ios
Android
Symbian
iPad Os
Ubuntu Touch

Mobile operating systems like iOS and Android are currently dominant in the smartphone and tablet market, with millions of devices running these platforms worldwide. These mobile companies include Apple, Samsung, Xiaomi, Huawei, Asus, and many others that produce smartphones and other mobile devices with these operating systems. Some of the reasons why these operating systems are relevant today are:

1. User base and popularity: iOS and Android have a massive user base, and the vast majority of smartphones and tablets worldwide run these operating systems. They are also backed up by major tech companies like Apple and Google, respectively, making them more reliable in terms of software updates, security patches, and app development.

2. App ecosystem: Both iOS and Android have a vast selection of apps available on their respective app stores, making it easy for users to find and install the apps they need for work, entertainment, and productivity.

3. Features and customization: These operating systems also come with advanced features like voice assistants, biometric authentication, and seamless integration with other devices and services. Users can personalize their devices by changing themes, icons, and wallpapers.

4. Open-source nature: While iOS and Android are proprietary platforms, there are also open-source mobile operating systems like Ubuntu Touch and Symbian that allow users to gain more control over their devices and customize their experience further.

5. Specific device use cases: For instance, iPad OS is designed to provide users with a desktop-like experience on larger screens, while Symbian is still in use for some older devices. These specific use cases make these operating systems relevant and useful for certain niches or industries.

What is Linux?
Type 1 hypervisor
UNIX-like operating system
OS kernel for many free distributions
OS X Mac subsystem

Linux is a UNIX-like operating system, which is known for being fast, stable, secure, and customizable. It is based on the Linux kernel, which is an integral part of many free and open-source operating systems like Ubuntu, Debian, Fedora, and many others. Linux is widely used in servers, desktop computers, smartphones, Internet of Things (IoT) devices, and other embedded systems. It is not a type 1 hypervisor, an OS X Mac subsystem, or an OS kernel only.

What is Linux? (answer with numbers)
1. Type 1 hypervisor
2. UNIX-like operating system
3. OS kernel for many free distributions
4. OS X Mac subsystem

Linux is a UNIX-like operating system, that uses the Linux kernel, and is the OS kernel for many free and open-source distributions. Therefore, the correct answer is "2. UNIX-like operating system" and "3. OS kernel for many free distributions". Linux is not a type 1 hypervisor or an OS X Mac subsystem.

Specify common features of modern mobile operating systems:  (answer with numbers)
1. Support for the x86 architecture
2. Voice assistant
3. Ability to connect an external monitor
4. Device search service
5. Backup user data to the cloud

The common features of modern mobile operating systems are:

2. Voice assistant
4. Device search service
5. Backup user data to the cloud

Support for the x86 architecture is not relevant for most mobile operating systems since they run on ARM-based processors, and the ability to connect an external monitor depends on the device and the type of port available.

Which operating system is Mac OS a copycat?
Linux
Windows
UNIX
Android

Mac OS is not a copycat of any operating system. However, like most modern operating systems, it shares some similarities with Unix-based systems. Mac OS has its own unique features, design, and functionality, and is developed exclusively for Apple hardware. The relationship between Mac OS and Unix stems from the fact that Mac OS is built on a Unix-based foundation and includes components of Unix-like systems such as FreeBSD and OpenBSD.

Select the criteria for the relevance of the operating system (OS):  (answer with numbers)
1. The OS can be run in hypervisors
2. Updates and fixes are coming out for the OS
3. For OC, there are drivers for processors based on the ARM architecture
4. There are a large number of different applications for this OS

The criteria for the relevance of an operating system (OS) are:

2. Updates and fixes are coming out for the OS
3. For OC, there are drivers for processors based on the ARM architecture
4. There are a large number of different applications for this OS

The ability to run in hypervisors is not a major criterion for relevance since not all users run operating systems in virtualized environments.
